originalTitle: De Zerbi reaffirms commitment to Spurs as relegation battle comes to conclusion
summary: Roberto De Zerbi has warned Tottenham’s players that their futures at the club are on the line against Everton on Sunday
content: Roberto De Zerbi has warned Tottenham’s players that their futures at the club are on the line against Everton on Sunday and reaffirmed his commitment to stay on if they are relegated for the first time since 1977. A point for Spurs would almost certainly mean they finish above West Ham thanks to their superior goal difference, even if West Ham beat Leeds in their final game. Having missed the chance to seal safety after suffering a first defeat in five matches, against Chelsea in midweek, Tottenham know that any slip-ups could prove disastrous. Spurs have lost 10 matches at home in the Premier League this season and have not won at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium since the start of December. De Zerbi recognised that his players’ confidence is fragile, although the Italian insisted they are all aware of what is at stake. “No, absolutely false. No, no, no, it’s not true,” he said when it was put to him that they do not care enough. “Maybe [it’s] totally different, maybe they suffered too much. Their reaction has been not right, but not for that reason because they are sensitive guys. “Maybe they didn’t know how they can go out of this situation. But there is just one solution to go out: to be strong, to be stronger than the atmosphere of the situation, or the injuries, or the negative things.” Asked if they are playing for their futures, he said: “For sure. Every one of us, we are playing for the future of Tottenham.” De Zerbi said it was his decision to arrange for the team to spend the night together before the Everton game and arrive at the stadium on the team bus “to create the right atmosphere”. “I want to put the heads on ice,” he said. “The secret is to manage the pressure. To manage the pressure you have to be clear and honest with yourself. The level of difficulty of the game is high. “We are fighting for something very important for everyone. But it is football. But we have enough quality. To attack the pressure you have to find the valour inside of yourself, to understand the situation and force yourself to give your best.” The former Brighton manager signed a five-year contract at the end of March after replacing the interim head coach, Igor Tudor. He said at the time that he wanted to stay even if Spurs went down, and nothing has changed. “It’s still an honour to be a coach for Tottenham, even if on Sunday we play for the relegation fight, it’s not a problem. I consider football something more than the [league] table.”
chineseTitle: 保级大战结束，德泽尔比重申对热刺的承诺
chineseSummary: 罗伯托·德泽尔比警告托特纳姆热刺球员，他们在俱乐部的未来将在周日对阵埃弗顿的比赛中受到威胁。
chineseContent: 罗伯托·德泽尔比警告托特纳姆热刺球员，周日对阵埃弗顿的比赛中，他们在俱乐部的未来岌岌可危，并重申，如果热刺自1977年以来首次降级，他将继续留队。热刺若能拿到一分，几乎肯定意味着他们凭借巨大的净胜球优势领先于西汉姆联，即使西汉姆联队在最后一场比赛中击败了利兹联队。托特纳姆热刺队在周中对阵切尔西队的比赛中遭遇五场比赛以来的首场失利，错失了确保安全的机会，他们知道任何失误都可能带来灾难性的后果。热刺本赛季在英超联赛主场已经输掉了10场比赛，并且自12月初以来在托特纳姆热刺球场还没有取得过胜利。德泽尔比承认他的球员的信心很脆弱，尽管意大利人坚称他们都知道什么是利害攸关的。 “不，绝对是假的。不，不，不，这不是真的，”当有人问他说他们不够关心时，他说道。 “也许完全不同，也许他们承受了太多痛苦。他们的反应不对，但不是因为这个原因，因为他们是敏感的人。“也许他们不知道如何摆脱这种情况。但走出去只有一个解决办法：坚强，比当时的气氛、伤病或消极的事情更坚强。”当被问及他们是否为自己的未来而战时，他说：“当然。我们每个人都在为托特纳姆热刺的未来而战。”德泽尔比表示，他决定安排球队在对阵埃弗顿的比赛前一起过夜，并乘坐球队巴士抵达体育场，“以创造合适的氛围”。 “我想把他们的头放在冰上，”他说。 “秘诀就是控制压力。为了应对压力，你必须对自己保持清晰和诚实。难度级别
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
